transcript: What's going on guys? Shane here. Welcome to my backyard. Just kidding, but someday. All right, so today we're going to be doing a home boxing or mixed martial arts workout. That's up to you. If you want to incorporate your kicks, knees, and elbows, or if you just want to do strictly hands, this is going to be a muscle endurance and cardio workout, all right? Here's the format. We're going to be doing three-minute rounds with 30-second rest in between for five rounds. If you want to do more or less, that's fine. If you want to tweak it in any way, that's okay too. It's going to be 30 seconds of shadowboxing. We'll break down how exactly you're going to shadowbox. Then 30 seconds of burpees, back to the shadowboxing. Then we're going to do 180 jump squats, back to shadowboxing again, and then we're going to finish with matrix push-ups, and we'll explain what those are. So we're starting it off with shadowboxing, which is a great full-body workout. It gets the heart rate up, and it improves your fighting skills. So if you're new to shadowboxing, a good rule of thumb is to finish your punch combinations with kicks or with knees. You can see I threw a jab and I finished with the opposite side knee. I finished with a cross and then I finished with the opposite side knee again. It could be a front push kick, it could be a round kick, or if you're just exclusively doing your punches, then just work your hands, all right? Visualize an opponent in front of you moving around so you're exercising good footwork and you're keeping your strikes accurate going to the head going to the body you're also going to work your defense your head movement your evasions sometimes sprint it out with some quick uppercuts or a clinch knees as soon as you're done you're going right to burpees all right 30 seconds of burpees non-stop rep them out as many as you can do make sure you're doing a push-up at the bottom fast hips exploding a jump at the top land soft down into your squat right back down burpees one of the best full body calisthenics that you can do again it gets the heart rate up it builds up the chest the shoulders the back as well as the core and the legs all right so times you want to give up or when i need you to especially keep on pushing forward all right that's when you're going to start to see results when you're pushing through that wall that you normally give up at as soon as you're done you're going to go right shadow boxing, all right? You're going to be fatigued. You're going to be tired in a fight too. Reach those goals that you're aiming for, whether it's to be a better fighter or to lose weight, get in shape. These are the times that you need to go hard. When you're huffing and puffing and when your limbs are tired, that's when you need to keep those hands up. You need to keep throwing with power, 100%, speed and accuracy, good footwork, switching it up. You need to be alert. You need to be mentally strong too, making sure you have a good strategy going to the head to the body working your kicks if this is kickboxing and sprawls if this is mma work that takedown defense next we've got 180 jump squats and these are killers here all right you're going to jump up spin 180 degrees midair so it's a great plyometric exercise here it's going to really burn the legs out you're going to feel this quick all right so you're going to jump you're going to land soft on your toes back down into a squat you can see me already fatiguing i'm getting tired here my legs are already on fire from squats the day before but no days off got to keep on working keep on pushing throw just like you guys you got 10 more seconds to keep on pushing through keep fighting through the pain all right embrace it that's what i always say if you feel the burn in your legs means you're doing something good then we're back to our shadow boxing and again you're really going to be fatigued after those jump squats but you got to keep on moving good footwork keep throwing those kicks this is the third and final round of this set so we got to finish strong finishing your punches with kicks triggering them up with feints maybe getting fancy with back kicks and guys if you don't know how to chain combos together you're not comfortable doing it no worries check out myfightgym.com we made it with the intention to teach you guys how to fight, how to feel more comfortable developing strategies so that you can chain combos together using taekwondo, muay thai, boxing, wrestling. All right. All right. Finishing it off now is the matrix pushups. These are tough and these can always be modified, but what you're doing is leaning to one side, one arm to the left or to the right. You're going to go down in a full circle motion. Once you get to the top, you're going to reverse and go in the opposite direction. Like I said, modify these by going down onto your knees or by just doing regular push-ups, okay? Work your way up to these if they're too difficult at first, all right? 30 seconds. Again, this is about muscle endurance. It's working the triceps. It's working the forearms, the chest, the back. Keep the core tight, the legs straight. And then once you're done, you got a 30-second rest and then you're going to go right back into back into it try to get five sets you'll be happy that you did it you can switch out different exercises different calisthenics for what works for you and just set a timer on your phone or watch a clock as you do it have fun with it work hard guys for more full-length workouts drills and techniques check out that link right in the middle there and the links also in the description below for my fight gym like i said we'll teach you how to shadowbox better and we'll give you full workouts that you can follow along with with a timer just like this one but that go up to 30 minutes in length until next time be sure to follow me on twitter and instagram and subscribe if you haven't already to get the fight tips before your opponent does i'm shane with fight tips self defense for the underdogs
